title: Siyu Chen (陈思羽)
text:
Siyu Chen
陈思羽



Undergraduate Researcher in Psychology

Meta-Self Lab · Nanjing Normal University


Computational Modeling · Artificial Neural Networks · Cognitive Decision-Making





📄
Publications


🔬
Projects


💬
Contact



📄 Download My CV


Hi, my friend! I’m glad you found your way to my website!
I’m Siyu Chen, an undergraduate student in Psychology， currently working in the Meta-Self Lab under the supervision of Hu Chuan-Peng. My interests mainly focus on Artificial Intelligence for Psychology during this phase. Specifically, I’m doing some research explorations of Computational Modeling based on Artificial Neural Networks, to train a model behaving like a human (which is super fun, isn’t it?), trying to uncover the cognitive mechanism of decision-making processes. Besides, I’m also fond of music and shooting photos, fascinated by philosophy, and have passion for exploring the ground truth of this world. Exercising like boxing, climbing and running are in my daily life as well.
I try to do research in a way that is transparent, reproducible, and inclusive. This may include things like publishing preprints, sharing data and code, and contributing to open source software development.
Feel free to take a look around and contact me if you have any thoughts or find any errors, hopefully we can make something great together!


Last updated: March 2026

title: Research Projects
text:
Ongoing Projects


A Cognitive Mechanism Exploring of Fast-Same Effect
Ongoing 2024 - 2026
Description: This project aims to build a neural network model incorporating cognitive processes to simulate the fast-same effect in matching tasks and explore its underlying cognitive mechanisms. The fast-same effect refers to the phenomenon where participants respond faster to “same” trials compared to “different” trials in matching tasks. By developing an artificial neural network model based on decision-making processes, we seek to better understand the cognitive mechanisms driving this effect.
My Role: - Designing and implementing neural network architectures for simulating the fast-same effect - Training and validating models against human behavioral data from matching tasks - Analyzing model performance and interpreting results to uncover cognitive mechanisms
Methods & Tools Used: - Python (PyTorch) - Artificial Neural Networks - Evidence Accumulation Models - Computational Modeling - Behavioral Data Analysis
Outcomes: - Resulting paper currently in preparation
Collaborators: - PI: Hu Chuan-Peng, School of Psychology, Nanjing Normal University



Research Interests


I am broadly interested in:

Cognitive Computational Modeling: Developing and fitting computational models to understand cognitive processes such as decision-making, learning, and memory.
Artificial Neural Networks: Using deep learning approaches to model human behavior and neural data, particularly in perceptual decision-making tasks.
Perceptual Decision-Making: Understanding how humans integrate sensory information to make decisions, and how factors like uncertainty and prior knowledge influence these decisions.
Open Science: Promoting transparent, reproducible, and inclusive research practices through preprints, open data, and open source software.



Last updated: March 2026
